label: performance_quadratic
summary: Logic is correct for small inputs — case normalization, empty-input handling, and ≥2 threshold are all right. The blocking defect is algorithmic: the implementation uses a string[] for deduplication (O(n) .includes scan) and a full second nested loop to count matches, producing O(n²) overall. Acceptance criterion 5 explicitly requires acceptable performance at 100k users; O(n²) at that scale runs in the order of minutes, not milliseconds. The fix is a straightforward one-pass Map<string, number> rewrite. Additionally, the test file listed in the completion signal does not appear in the diff, so test quality cannot be assessed. Both issues must be resolved before merge.

findings.description: seen is declared as string[] and queried via .includes(), which is O(n) per iteration; combined with the inner counting loop this makes the function O(n²) — catastrophically slow for 100k users (≈10B comparisons, ~100s in JS).
findings.suggestion: Replace both the seen-deduplication and counting logic with a single Map<string, number> pass: iterate once, increment counts, then filter entries with count >= 2. O(n) time, O(n) space.

findings.description: Completion signal lists tests/users/duplicates.test.ts in filesChanged but this file is absent from the diff — test coverage and correctness cannot be verified from the review materials.
findings.suggestion: Include the test file in the diff or confirm it was committed; at minimum verify a test covers the 100k performance bound.

Acceptance criterion 5 explicitly requires acceptable performance at 100k users; O(n²) at that scale runs in the order of minutes, not milliseconds. The fix is a straightforward one-pass Map<string, number> rewrite. Additionally, the test file listed in the completion signal does not appear in the diff, so test quality cannot be assessed. criteriaAssessment.evidence: export function detectDuplicates(...) is present at the top level of src/users/duplicates.ts.

criteriaAssessment.criterion: Returns array of { email, count } for emails appearing 2+ times
criteriaAssessment.evidence: Result type is Duplicate[] ({ email: string; count: number }), pushed only when count >= 2.
Empty users array — outer for-of loop body never executes, result stays [], returned at line 25.

criteriaAssessment.criterion: Handles case-insensitive email matching (foo@x.com and FOO@X.COM are duplicates)
criteriaAssessment.evidence: All comparisons use user.email.toLowerCase(); normalized value stored in result.email.
Nested loop structure is O(n²): outer loop × inner counting loop, plus O(n) seen.includes() on each outer iteration. At n=100,000 this is ≈10 billion operations — not acceptable.
